content.full_text:
David C. Garner 
375 Hazel Street 
Gridley, CA 95948 
... 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento. CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
October 12, 2012 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-12-153 
Dear Mr. Gamer: 
This letter is in response to your request for written advice of October 10, 2012. Your 
inquiry primarily presents issues which are outside the purview of the Political Reform Act 
("Act") . Your question specifically concerns Health and Safety Code section 33130(a) which 
provides that no "agency or community officer or employee who in the course of his or her 
duties is required to participate in the formulation of, or to approve plans or policies for, the 
redevelopment of a project area shall acquire any interest in any property included within a 
project area within the community." 
This section is not in the Political Reform Act and we cannot advise on it. Thus, based 
on Regulation l8329(b)(8)(D), copy enclosed, since your request does not meet the requirements 
for formal written advice, the request must be declined by this agency. 
Should you need assistance in the future, you may contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
 
JWW:jgl 
1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear al title 2. sections 18109-
18996, of the California code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: This section is not in the Political Reform Act and we cannot advise on it. Thus, based on Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), since your request does not meet the requirements for formal written advice, the request must be declined by this agency.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked about whether Health and Safety Code section 33130(a) restrictions on acquiring property interests in redevelopment project areas apply to a particular situation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the inquiry concerns Health and Safety Code section 33130(a), which falls outside the jurisdiction of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear explanation of why advice cannot be provided. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but requestor's inquiry and FPPC's declination reasoning are clearly stated in body text.

QUESTION: The requestor asked about whether Health and Safety Code section 33130(a) restrictions on acquiring property interests in redevelopment project areas apply to a particular situation.

CONCLUSION: This section is not in the Political Reform Act and we cannot advise on it. Thus, based on Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), since your request does not meet the requirements for formal written advice, the request must be declined by this agency.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for written advice from David C. Garner regarding Health and Safety Code section 33130(a) restrictions on property acquisition by redevelopment agency employees, as this statute falls outside the Political Reform Act's scope.
